corrected_fields.macro_context: This release provides data on consumer spending expectations from the December 2021 Survey of Consumer Expectations. Rising spending growth expectations (4.6% year-ahead) combined with lower reported likelihood of large purchases suggests households anticipate elevated prices, which could reinforce inflationary pressures. Historical record from early post-pandemic period (January 2022) — serves as a benchmark for consumer behavior during that era. Topic note: SOURCE TARGET 'market structure' is misaligned with this content type; consumer spending expectations data would be better categorized under 'macro catalysts' or 'economic data'.
